14def _parse_line(line):
15    fields = []
16    field = []
17    in_quotes = False
18    i = 0
19    n = len(line)
20    while i < n:
21        c = line[i]
22        if in_quotes:
23            if c == '"':
24                if i + 1 < n and line[i + 1] == '"':
25                    field.append('"')
26                    i += 2
27                    continue
28                in_quotes = False
29                i += 1
30                continue
31            field.append(c)
32            i += 1
33        else:
34            if c == '"':
35                in_quotes = True
36                i += 1
37            elif c == ",":
38                fields.append("".join(field))
39                field = []
40                i += 1
41            else:
42                field.append(c)
43                i += 1
44    fields.append("".join(field))
45    return fields

 >lue

tt14 
15 
16def _parse_line(line):
17    fields = []
18    i = 0
19    n = len(line)
20    while i < n:
21        if line[i] == '"':
22            # Quoted field
23            i += 1
24            buf = []
25            while i < n:
26                if line[i] == '"':
27                    if i + 1 < n and line[i + 1] == '"':
28                        buf.append('"')
29                        i += 2
30                    else:
31                        i += 1
32                        break
33                else:
34                    buf.append(line[i])
35                    i += 1
36            fields.append(''.join(buf))
37            if i < n and line[i] == ',':
38                i += 1
39                if i == n:
40                    fields.append('')
41            elif i < n:
42                # Unexpected characters after closing quote; treat them as
43                # part of the same field (unquoted continuation).
44                start = i
45                while i < n and line[i] != ',':
46                    i += 1
47                fields[-1] += line[start:i]
48                if i < n and line[i] == ',':
49                    i += 1
50                    if i == n:
51                        fields.append('')
52        else:
53            # Unquoted field
54            start = i
55            while i < n and line[i] != ',':
56                i += 1
57            fields.append(line[start:i])
58            if i < n and line[i] == ',':
59                i += 1
60                if i == n:
61                    fields.append('')
62    return fields

4    Blank lines are skipped. Fields are separated by commas.4    Blank lines are skipped. Fields are separated by commas.
nn5    Quoted fields are wrapped in double quotes. Inside a quoted field,
6    commas are part of the value, and two double quotes ("") represent
7    a single literal double quote.
5    """8    """
6    rows = []9    rows = []
7    for line in text.splitlines():10    for line in text.splitlines():
8        if line == "":11        if line == "":
9            continue12            continue
t10        rows.append(line.split(","))t13        fields = []
14        i = 0
15        while i < len(line):
16            if line[i] == '"':
17                # Quoted field
18                i += 1
19                field_chars = []
20                while i < len(line):
21                    if line[i] == '"':
22                        if i + 1 < len(line) and line[i+1] == '"':
23                            field_chars.append('"')
24                            i += 2
25                        else:
26                            # Closing quote
27                            i += 1
28                            break
29                    else:
30                        field_chars.append(line[i])
31                        i += 1
32                fields.append(''.join(field_chars))
33                # Skip comma separator if present
34                if i < len(line) and line[i] == ',':
35                    i += 1
36            else:
37                # Unquoted field
38                field_chars = []
39                while i < len(line) and line[i] != ',':
40                    field_chars.append(line[i])
41                    i += 1
42                fields.append(''.join(field_chars))
43                if i < len(line) and line[i] == ',':
44                    i += 1
45        # Handle trailing empty field (line ends with a comma)
46        if line.endswith(','):
47            fields.append('')
48        rows.append(fields)
11    return rows49    return rows

nn1import csv
2import io
3 
1def parse_csv(text):4def parse_csv(text):
2    """Parse CSV text into a list of rows, where each row is a list of field5    """Parse CSV text into a list of rows, where each row is a list of field
>s.>s.
36
4    Blank lines are skipped. Fields are separated by commas.7    Blank lines are skipped. Fields are separated by commas.
5    """8    """
6    rows = []9    rows = []
nn10    # Use io.StringIO to treat the string as a file for the csv module
11    stream = io.StringIO(text)
12    reader = csv.reader(stream, quotechar='"', delimiter=',', quoting=csv.QU
 >OTE_MINIMAL)
13    
14    for row in reader:
15        # Skip completely blank lines (which the csv reader might return as 
 >empty lists)
16        # Since the task specifically mentions blank lines in the input text
 >,
17        # and csv.reader might produce an empty list for an empty line.
18        if not row:
19            continue
20            
21        # Note: csv.reader handles the quoted fields and escaped quotes ("")
 > automatically
22        rows.append(row)
23        
24    # The csv module can be slightly inconsistent with splitlines() behavior
 > on trailing newlines
25    # If the original text had a blank line at the start or end, we need to 
 >ensure we match 
26    # the "Blank lines are skipped" requirement.
27    
28    # Re-evaluating based on the splitlines requirement from the original co
 >de:
29    # To strictly follow "Blank lines are skipped" and handle the CSV logic:
30    final_rows = []
31    for line in text.splitlines():
32        if not line.strip() and not line: # genuinely blank
33            continue
34        if line == "":
35            continue
36        # Use csv.reader on a single line to maintain the "skip blank lines"
 > logic 
37        # from the original implementation while gaining CSV parsing capabil
 >ities.
38        single_line_reader = csv.reader([line], quotechar='"', delimiter=','
 >, quoting=csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL)
39        for row in single_line_reader:
40            final_rows.append(row)
41

nn14  // Parse and validate page and limit

33  }
34 
35  const start = (page - 1) * limit;

38

nn1DISCOUNT_RATES = {
2    "vip": 0.10,
3    "member": 0.05,
4}
5 
6FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD = 100
7SHIPPING_COST = 10
8TAX_RATE = 0.08
9 
10 
11def _compute_subtotal(items):
12    return sum(it["price"] * it["qty"] for it in items)
13 
14 
15def _apply_customer_discount(subtotal, customer_type):
16    rate = DISCOUNT_RATES.get(customer_type, 0)
17    return subtotal * (1 - rate)
18 
19 
20def _apply_coupon(amount, coupon):
21    if coupon == "SAVE5":
22        return max(amount - 5, 0), False
23    if coupon == "FREESHIP":
24        return amount, True
25    return amount, False
26 
27 
28def _compute_shipping(amount, free_ship):
29    if free_ship or amount >= FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD:
30        return 0
31    return SHIPPING_COST
32 
33 
1def process_order(items, customer_type, coupon):34def process_order(items, customer_type, coupon):
n2    # compute subtotaln35    subtotal = _compute_subtotal(items)
3    subtotal = 036    after_discount = _apply_customer_discount(subtotal, customer_type)
4    for it in items:37    after_coupon, free_ship = _apply_coupon(after_discount, coupon)
5        subtotal = subtotal + it["price"] * it["qty"]
638
n7    # figure out discount based on customer typen39    shipping = _compute_shipping(after_coupon, free_ship)
8    if customer_type == "vip":
9        after_discount = subtotal - subtotal * 0.10
10    else:
11        if customer_type == "member":
12            after_discount = subtotal - subtotal * 0.05
13        else:
14            after_discount = subtotal
15 
16    # apply coupon
17    if coupon is not None:
18        if coupon == "SAVE5":
19            after_coupon = after_discount - 5
20            if after_coupon < 0:
21                after_coupon = 0
22            free_ship = False
23        elif coupon == "FREESHIP":
24            after_coupon = after_discount
25            free_ship = True
26        else:
27            after_coupon = after_discount
28            free_ship = False
29    else:
30        after_coupon = after_discount
31        free_ship = False
32 
33    # shipping
34    if free_ship:
35        shipping = 0
36    else:
37        if after_coupon >= 100:
38            shipping = 0
39        else:
40            shipping = 10
41 
42    # tax
43    tax = after_coupon * 0.0840    tax = after_coupon * TAX_RATE
44 
45    # total
46    total = after_coupon + tax + shipping41    total = after_coupon + tax + shipping
4742
t48    result = {}t43    return {
49    result["subtotal"] = round(subtotal, 2)44        "subtotal": round(subtotal, 2),
50    result["discount"] = round(subtotal - after_coupon, 2)45        "discount": round(subtotal - after_coupon, 2),

projects/05-god-refactor/pricing.py

main
opus
nn1CUSTOMER_DISCOUNT_RATES = {
2    "vip": 0.10,
3    "member": 0.05,
4}
5 
6FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD = 100
7STANDARD_SHIPPING = 10
8TAX_RATE = 0.08
9 
10 
11def _subtotal(items):
12    return sum(it["price"] * it["qty"] for it in items)
13 
14 
15def _apply_customer_discount(subtotal, customer_type):
16    rate = CUSTOMER_DISCOUNT_RATES.get(customer_type, 0)
17    return subtotal - subtotal * rate
18 
19 
20def _apply_coupon(amount, coupon):
21    """Return (amount_after_coupon, free_shipping)."""
22    if coupon == "SAVE5":
23        return max(amount - 5, 0), False
24    if coupon == "FREESHIP":
25        return amount, True
26    return amount, False

29def _shipping(amount, free_ship):
30    if free_ship or amount >= FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD:
31        return 0
32    return STANDARD_SHIPPING

f1def process_order(items, customer_type, coupon):f1def process_order(items, customer_type, coupon):
n2    # compute subtotaln2    subtotal = _calculate_subtotal(items)

5        subtotal = subtotal + it["price"] * it["qty"]5    shipping = _calculate_shipping(after_coupon, free_ship)
6 6    tax = _calculate_tax(after_coupon)
7    # figure out discount based on customer type
8    if customer_type == "vip":
9        after_discount = subtotal - subtotal * 0.10
10    else:
11        if customer_type == "member":
12            after_discount = subtotal - subtotal * 0.05
13        else:
14            after_discount = subtotal
15 
16    # apply coupon
17    if coupon is not None:
18        if coupon == "SAVE5":
19            after_coupon = after_discount - 5
20            if after_coupon < 0:
21                after_coupon = 0
22            free_ship = False
23        elif coupon == "FREESHIP":
24            after_coupon = after_discount
25            free_ship = True
26        else:
27            after_coupon = after_discount
28            free_ship = False
29    else:
30        after_coupon = after_discount
31        free_ship = False
32 
33    # shipping
34    if free_ship:
35        shipping = 0
36    else:
37        if after_coupon >= 100:
38            shipping = 0
39        else:
40            shipping = 10
41 
42    # tax
43    tax = after_coupon * 0.08
44 
45    # total

478
t48    result = {}t9    return _build_result(subtotal, after_coupon, tax, shipping, total)
10 
11 
12def _calculate_subtotal(items):
13    return sum(item["price"] * item["qty"] for item in items)
14 
15 
16def _apply_discount(subtotal, customer_type):
17    discount_rates = {
18        "vip": 0.10,
19        "member": 0.05,
20    }
21    rate = discount_rates.get(customer_type, 0)
22    return subtotal - subtotal * rate
23 
24 
25def _apply_coupon(amount, coupon):
26    if coupon == "SAVE5":
27        return max(0, amount - 5), False
28    elif coupon == "FREESHIP":
29        return amount, True
30    else:
31        return amount, False
32 
33 
34def _calculate_shipping(amount, free_ship):
35    if free_ship or amount >= 100:
36        return 0
37    return 10
38 
39 
40def _calculate_tax(amount):
41    return amount * 0.08
42 
43 
44def _build_result(subtotal, after_coupon, tax, shipping, total):
45    return {
49    result["subtotal"] = round(subtotal, 2)46        "subtotal": round(subtotal, 2),
50    result["discount"] = round(subtotal - after_coupon, 2)47        "discount": round(subtotal - after_coupon, 2),
51    result["tax"] = round(tax, 2)48        "tax": round(tax, 2),
52    result["shipping"] = round(shipping, 2)49        "shipping": round(shipping, 2),
53    result["total"] = round(total, 2)50        "total": round(total, 2),
54    return result51    }

35// lock-ordering deadlocks, the two account mutexes are always acquired in a
36// consistent order (by account name).
35func (b *Bank) Transfer(from, to string, amount int) {37func (b *Bank) Transfer(from, to string, amount int) {
36        f := b.accounts[from]38        f := b.accounts[from]
37        t := b.accounts[to]39        t := b.accounts[to]
tt40 
41        if from == to {
42                // No movement between distinct accounts; still lock to keep
 > semantics.
43                f.mu.Lock()
44                defer f.mu.Unlock()
45                f.balance -= amount
46                f.balance += amount
47                return
48        }
49 
50        // Acquire locks in a consistent, total order so that concurrent tra
 >nsfers
51        // in opposite directions cannot deadlock.
52        first, second := f, t
53        if from > to {
54                first, second = t, f
55        }
56 
38        f.mu.Lock()57        first.mu.Lock()

16def match(text, pattern):16def match(text, pattern):
17    """Return True iff the whole of `text` matches `pattern`."""17    """Return True iff the whole of `text` matches `pattern`."""
n18    return _match(text, 0, pattern, 0)n18    # Use dynamic programming to avoid exponential time complexity.
19    # dp[ti] will be True if the prefix of text up to ti matches the current
20    # processed part of the pattern.
21    
22    # Pre-process the pattern into a list of "tokens" to avoid re-parsing
23    # class brackets inside the DP loop.
24    tokens = []
25    pi = 0
26    while pi < len(pattern):
27        c = pattern[pi]
28        if c == '*':
29            tokens.append('*')
30            pi += 1
31        elif c == '?':
32            tokens.append('?')
33            pi += 1
34        elif c == '[':
35            # Handle the complex class bracket logic
36            i = pi + 1
37            negate = i < len(pattern) and pattern[i] == "!"
38            if negate:
39                i += 1
40            chars = []
41            if i < len(pattern) and pattern[i] == "]":
42                chars.append("]")
43                i += 1
44            while i < len(pattern) and pattern[i] != "]":
45                chars.append(pattern[i])
46                i += 1
47            
48            if i == len(pattern):
49                # Unterminated '[': matches literal '['
50                tokens.append(('lit', '['))
51                pi += 1
52            else:
53                # Set match: store the set and the negation flag
54                tokens.append(('set', set(chars), negate))
55                pi = i + 1
56        else:
57            tokens.append(('lit', c))
58            pi += 1

 >ken_idx]
61    current_matches = [False] * (len(text) + 1)
62    current_matches[0] = True

n21def _match(t, ti, p, pi):n64    for token in tokens:

36    return False79        elif isinstance(token, tuple):

39def _match_class(ch, p, pi):
40    """p[pi] == '['. Return (does ch match the class, index after the class)
>.""" 
41    i = pi + 1
42    negate = i < len(p) and p[i] == "!"
43    if negate:
44        i += 1
45    chars = []
46    if i < len(p) and p[i] == "]":
47        chars.append("]")
48        i += 1
49    while i < len(p) and p[i] != "]":
50        chars.append(p[i])
51        i += 1
52    if i == len(p):
53        return ch == "[", pi + 1
54    return (ch in chars) != negate, i + 1

16def match(text, pattern):16def match(text, pattern):
17    """Return True iff the whole of `text` matches `pattern`."""17    """Return True iff the whole of `text` matches `pattern`."""
n18    return _match(text, 0, pattern, 0)n18    n = len(text)
19    m = len(pattern)
20 
21    # Identify token starts in the pattern.  A class like [abc] is one token
22    # that spans multiple pattern positions; positions inside it are not
23    # token starts.  For each token start we also store the index just past
24    # the token (token_end) and, for class tokens, the membership info.
25    token_end = [0] * m
26    class_info = [None] * m          # (negate, members_set) for class token
 >s
27    is_token_start = [False] * m
28 
29    j = 0
30    while j < m:
31        is_token_start[j] = True
32        if pattern[j] == '[':
33            unterminated, negate, members, end = _parse_class(pattern, j)
34            if unterminated:
35                # The '[' is a literal character; the rest of the pattern
36                # is tokenized normally.
37                token_end[j] = j + 1
38                j += 1
39            else:
40                token_end[j] = end
41                class_info[j] = (negate, members)
42                j = end
43        else:
44            token_end[j] = j + 1
45            j += 1
46 
47    # Dynamic programming over suffixes.
48    # next_row corresponds to dp[i+1][*]; we build dp[i][*] into cur.
49    next_row = bytearray(m + 1)
50    for i in range(n, -1, -1):
51        cur = bytearray(m + 1)
52        if i == n:
53            cur[m] = 1
54 
55        for j in range(m - 1, -1, -1):
56            if not is_token_start[j]:
57                continue
58 
59            c = pattern[j]
60            if c == '*':
61                # '*' matches either an empty sequence or one more character
 >.
62                if cur[j + 1] or (i < n and next_row[j]):
63                    cur[j] = 1
64            elif c == '?':
65                if i < n:
66                    cur[j] = next_row[j + 1]
67            elif c == '[':
68                if i < n:
69                    info = class_info[j]
70                    if info is None:
71                        # Unterminated '[' matches a literal '['.
72                        if text[i] == '[':
73                            cur[j] = next_row[j + 1]
74                    else:
75                        negate, members = info
76                        if (text[i] in members) != negate:
77                            cur[j] = next_row[token_end[j]]
78            else:
79                if i < n and c == text[i]:
80                    cur[j] = next_row[j + 1]
81 
82        next_row = cur
83 
84    return bool(next_row[0])

t21def _match(t, ti, p, pi):t87def _parse_class(p, pi):
88    """p[pi] == '['.  Return (unterminated, negate, members, index_after_cla
 >ss).
89 
90    If the class is unterminated, the caller should treat the '[' as a
91    literal character of length 1.
92    """
93    i = pi + 1
94    negate = i < len(p) and p[i] == '!'
95    if negate:
96        i += 1
97 
98    members = set()
99    if i < len(p) and p[i] == ']':
100        members.add(']')
101        i += 1
102 
103    while i < len(p) and p[i] != ']':
104        members.add(p[i])
105        i += 1
106 
22    if pi == len(p):107    if i == len(p):
23        return ti == len(t)108        return (True, False, set(), pi + 1)
24    c = p[pi]109 
25    if c == "*":110    return (False, negate, members, i + 1)
26        return _match(t, ti, p, pi + 1) or (
27            ti < len(t) and _match(t, ti + 1, p, pi)
28        )
29    if ti == len(t):
30        return False
31    if c == "[":
32        ok, nxt = _match_class(t[ti], p, pi)
33        return ok and _match(t, ti + 1, p, nxt)
34    if c == "?" or c == t[ti]:
35        return _match(t, ti + 1, p, pi + 1)
36    return False
